A Long Weekend Proposal · UK

UK Weekend 🇬🇧

A short plan for a potential weekend in the UK — José bday edition!!!!

// all choices below are weather-dependent

First things firstGetting to Sinzi's place

Airports → Chiswick · time & money

AirportTravel timeCost
Heathrow25–45 min£5–£10
Gatwick1h–1h30£12–£30
Stansted1h30–2h30£20–£30
Luton1h–1h45£15–£35
Vibe Check

How are you feeling?

Move the sliders. I'll tell you what kind of weekend you're describing.

chill adventure intensity send it
all city city ↔ nature all nature
tent comfort level nice b&b
Verdict A balanced weekend — mix of movement and stillness, somewhere between roughing it and cosy.

Idea 01 South Coast Combo Recommended

Climbing + coast + optional camping

Day 0 · Thursday eveningArrival in London

  • Transfer from airport to Chiswick (see airports above)
  • Easy evening
  • Light planning

Day 1 · FridayFlexible London Day

Option 1 — easing into it

  • Work during the day
  • Half-day wander around London
  • Potential activities (e.g. IMAX film, food, general roaming)

Option 2 — leave the city fast

  • Leave London Friday afternoon
  • Drive towards the coast
  • Sit in more traffic than usual

Day 2 · SaturdayClimbing Day (aka fixing past mistakes)

→ Isle of Portland
  • Sport climbing on limestone sea cliffs
  • Route selection based on confidence levels
  • Reconsidering whether climbing is for me, or whether I'm more of a spectator

Evening

  • Camp
  • B&B
  • Hostel

Day 3 · SundayCoastal Walk + Light Exploration

→ Jurassic Coast · Durdle Door → Lulworth Cove hike
  • Coastal hike (3–5h, adjustable) — route reference
  • Cliff views, sea air
  • Fossil hunting

Return options

  • Sunday evening: back to London or straight to airport
  • Monday: more relaxed exit

Preferred: Monday (or indefinite extension of trip)

Pros

  • Relatively close to London
  • Easy to adjust depending on mood/energy/weather
  • Strong mix of climbing, movement, scenery
  • No overcomplicated logistics

Cons

  • Not "big mountain" dramatic
  • Weather may or may not cooperate
  • Camping decisions may be reassessed in real time
  • I haven't done lead belaying yet
· · · · ·

AlternativesOther Ideas

In case the South Coast doesn't land

Heads up — all of these imply long drives from London. Roughly 4–6h+ each way, so a chunk of the weekend goes on the road.

Idea 02 · Wales Snowdonia National Park · more rugged, more serious · ~5h drive

  • Scrambling, proper mountain feel

Idea 03 · Lake District Lake District National Park · classic UK mountains · ~5–6h drive

  • Ridge lines, trad climbing, bigger days

Idea 04 · Peak District Peak District National Park · climbing focus · ~4h drive

  • Gritstone climbing, shorter movement days

Closing Note

Current preferred option

  • Simplicity
  • Efficiency
  • High likelihood of a good time

Subject to change

  • Weather
  • Energy levels
  • General mood of the expedition
